purpose: Keep Easy Harness Visual Draft behavior stable across prompts, models, and new conversations.
global_must: Show that Easy Harness received all supplied materials.
Show the connection goal in customer language.
Build requirement_map_v0_1 before rendering the visual draft.
Preserve evidence boundaries for files that were only received or only lightly parsed.
Use requirement_map, fact_trace, evidence_coverage, question_plan, and draft_readiness.
Render the visible wiring poster from requirement_map as the primary source of endpoints, sections, connection groups, open items, and evidence.
Put engineering confirmation into Easy Harness review instead of pushing it onto the customer.
Never present the visual Draft as a final manufacturing package.
global_must_not: Do not ask a long questionnaire.
Do not pretend to read attachment content without observations.
Do not close Draft when the connection goal is missing.
Do not ask for exact connector part numbers, terminals, crimps, or wire gauges unless the customer explicitly has them and the Draft cannot proceed without them.
Do not let internal schema language dominate the customer-facing output.

cases.input: I need a short custom harness for a Deutsch DT06-6S connector. Use the attached connector image, PDF note, CSV pinout, XLSX table, and CAD reference files. Target length is about 450 mm. This will be used on a 12V automotive sensor branch. Please organize what I gave you into an Easy Harness Draft and ask me only for missing information.

required_question_meaning: What is the other end of the harness?

cases.input: We need 15 pcs of a harness for a battery test fixture. One controller box connects to four temperature sensors, two small cooling fans, and one emergency stop switch. Main trunk about 900 mm. Sensor drops about 250 mm. Fans are 24V but I do not know current. I uploaded photos of the controller connector, the old sample, and a spreadsheet with pin names. Please organize what you can and tell me only what is really needed.
must_understand: controller box connects to temperature sensors, cooling fans, and emergency stop switch
fans are 24V and current is unknown

allowed_topics: fan load current or device model if known
temperature sensor type or model if known

easy_harness_review_should_include: connector identification from photos or sample
wire gauge and load rating review
final connector and production detail review

cases.input: I uploaded CAD-style reference files for the connector shell, bracket outline, and protective boot. Please use them as dimensional/context references, not final manufacturing drawings.
must_understand: they are not final manufacturing drawings

question_policy.required_question_meaning: What should this harness or cable connect, copy, or replace?

cases.input: I want to copy an old harness for an outdoor sensor assembly. The old harness connects one controller box to two external sensors. Approximate total length is 1.2 m. Quantity 3 pcs first. It is signal wiring only and carries no motor or battery power. I have photos of both connector ends and the old sample.
must_understand: controller box to two external sensors
signal only, no motor or battery power

allowed_topics: whether the two sensor ends are identical or different

cases.input: The pin mapping is in my spreadsheet and CSV. Please use those files as the source of truth. Connector A is DT06-6S. I am not sure about wire colors for pins 5 and 6.
must_understand: pin mapping files are intended as source of truth
wire colors for pins 5 and 6 are uncertain
the other end is not stated

required_question_meaning: What is the other end of the harness?
What quantity and approximate length are needed?
